title: rosa × sabinii var. coronata
intro: Rosa × sabinii var. coronata, also known as the Sabin's Rose or Crowned Sabin's Rose, is a variety of flowering plant in the rose family, known for its fragrant, pink to white flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa × sabinii var. coronata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Rosa × sabinii var. coronata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in essential oil form, for its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
